boundary_setting.analytic_question: Would changing the unit, attachment, or scope boundary change the interpretation?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: The boundary is fixed before coding and no longer affects the decision.
A stable boundary leaves a perspective or warrant issue as dominant.
candidate_detection.question: Does uncertain unit, scope, category, episode, or speaker attachment plausibly control the result?
supported_if: Changing quotation attachment changes the interpretation.
Changing the case boundary changes which evidence counts.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the interpretive unit, category scope, or speaker attachment were fixed differently while standpoint stayed constant, would the proposed interpretation change?
Ask whether changing the boundary of the annotation would change the result; if the boundary is stable but warrants interact, use warrant_relation.
Ask whether the relevant unit/scope is unstable, or whether the unit is stable but needs external contextual inference to support conversion.
Hold standpoint constant and alter unit or speaker attachment; then hold attachment constant and alter standpoint. Which manipulation changes the result?

boundary_setting.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
boundary_setting.definition: Instability over the relevant case, interpretive unit, category boundary, speaker attachment, or scope of application is dominant.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when the unit is stable and a contextual bridge is needed; use context_inference.
Do not use for administrative segmentation that does not affect the decision.
Do not use when standpoint is stable but perspective controls the function; use perspective_assignment.
boundary_setting.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: The boundary is fixed before coding and no longer affects the decision.
A stable boundary leaves a perspective or warrant issue as dominant.
positive_indicators: Changing quotation attachment changes the interpretation.
Changing the case boundary changes which evidence counts.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the interpretive unit, category scope, or speaker attachment were fixed differently while standpoint stayed constant, would the proposed interpretation change?

boundary_setting.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table calls boundary_setting an operational value awaiting positive out-of-sample testing.
trigger: unit, speaker, category, case, or scope boundary controls the outcome
proposal is made by a model record

use_another_value_when: Use perspective_assignment when attachment is stable but standpoint, focalization, or witness role changes interpretation.
Use context_inference when unit/scope is stable but a named contextual bridge is required.
Use warrant_relation when locally available warrants interact inside a stable boundary.
use_when: The record depends on where the case begins or ends.
Speaker attachment, quotation attachment, category scope, or episode scope is unstable.
Different reasonable boundaries would produce different interpretations.

context_inference.analytic_question: Is the local evidence stable but insufficient without a cited contextual bridge permitted by the protocol?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: The bridge cannot be cited.
The bridge is actually a local warrant.
The bridge is outside the packet and the protocol does not permit its use.
candidate_detection.question: Is there positive evidence for a named contextual bridge that is necessary, cited, typed, and protocol-permitted?
supported_if: A genre convention is cited and needed to license conversion.
An institutional rule documented inside the packet or protocol-approved context bridges local evidence to interpretation.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the named contextual bridge were unavailable, would the proposed interpretation no longer be licensed by the local anchor?
Ask whether the relevant unit/scope is unstable, or whether the unit is stable but needs external contextual inference to support conversion.
Ask whether later readability is the decisive feature; if yes, use temporal_layering rather than context_inference.
Ask whether the local text contains interacting warrants; if yes, use warrant_relation. Use context_inference only when the missing contextual bridge is dominant.

context_inference.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
context_inference.definition: A named contextual bridge beyond the local anchor is the dominant positive support needed for evidence-to-interpretation conversion.
do_not_use_when: Do not use solely because no other category fits.
Do not use without naming the contextual bridge and where it is documented.
Do not use when locally available warrants interact; use warrant_relation.
context_inference.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: The bridge is actually a local warrant.
The bridge is outside the packet and the protocol does not permit its use.
positive_indicators: A genre convention is cited and needed to license conversion.
An institutional rule documented inside the packet or protocol-approved context bridges local evidence to interpretation.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the named contextual bridge were unavailable, would the proposed interpretation no longer be licensed by the local anchor?

context_inference.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table calls context_inference awaiting positive out-of-sample testing and vulnerable to residual use.
trigger: named contextual bridge is positively cited
proposal is made by a model record

use_another_value_when: Use boundary_setting when the relevant unit or scope itself is unstable.
Use temporal_layering when later textual framing reclassifies earlier evidence.
Use warrant_relation when local warrants carry the decision through interaction.
use_when: A specific contextual bridge is named and cited.
The bridge type is social, genre, institutional, historical, discourse, or another declared type.
The record explains why local evidence is insufficient without the bridge and why the bridge is context rather than a new warrant.

escalate_if: bridge is outside packet and protocol does not permit it
bridge appears to be a local warrant instead of context

cue_function.analytic_question: Is the unresolved conversion produced by what kind of cue this is rather than by who uses it, how it is used, how warrants relate, or what context is needed?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: A more specific locus remains supported.
The cue is only topic vocabulary.
candidate_detection.question: Does cue-family function itself remain a plausible source of procedural friction after more specific loci are evaluated?
supported_if: A cue family has more than one plausible procedural role.
A substitution of cue family changes the interpretation while other variables are held constant.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the same local content arrived through a different cue family, with speaker, operation, warrant, time, boundary, and context held stable, would the proposed interpretation change?
Ask whether the problem is what this cue type does here, or what an act of interpretation, consultation, confession, recognition, or self-reading does.
Ask whether the cue type itself is underdetermined after excluding more specific loci, or whether one source, sign, medium, speaker, or result is granted standing.

cue_function.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
cue_function.definition: The cue family itself remains the dominant procedural locus after more specific loci have been tested and excluded.
do_not_use_when: Do not use because a cue word appears in the passage.
Do not use when a visible interpretive act performs the conversion; use operation_function.
Do not use when one source, speaker, sign, or medium receives standing; use warrant_attribution.
cue_function.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: A more specific locus remains supported.
The cue is only topic vocabulary.
positive_indicators: A cue family has more than one plausible procedural role.
A substitution of cue family changes the interpretation while other variables are held constant.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the same local content arrived through a different cue family, with speaker, operation, warrant, time, boundary, and context held stable, would the proposed interpretation change?

cue_function.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table calls cue_function a provisional reserved value with no positive demo example.
trigger: cue family remains plausible after all more specific loci are tested
proposal is made by a model record
counterfactual tests do not support another specific locus

use_another_value_when: Use operation_function when the act of reading, recognizing, consulting, confessing, testifying, or interpreting performs conversion.
Use warrant_attribution when the issue is whether one source, sign, speaker, medium, or result has standing.
Use context_inference when a named contextual bridge, not cue family, licenses the conversion.
use_when: The cue type, such as omen, testimony, prophecy, sign, inscription, confession, or token, is itself unstable as a converter.
All more specific candidate loci have been evaluated and do not better explain the friction.
Changing the cue family while holding evidence content, standpoint, operation, warrant, time, boundary, and context stable would change the interpretation.

operation_function.analytic_question: Does an act performed in or by the record do the converting work?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: The act is physical but not interpretive.
The operation is mentioned but not needed for the decision.
candidate_detection.question: Does a visible interpretive, testimonial, consultative, recognitional, or self-reading operation plausibly perform conversion?
supported_if: A character interprets a sign and that act supplies the function.
A confession or recognition changes how evidence is read.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the visible operation were removed but the same evidence remained, would the proposed interpretation still follow?
Ask whether the problem is what this cue type does here, or what an act of interpretation, consultation, confession, recognition, or self-reading does.
If evidence becomes usable as a warrant through the operation, prefer warrant_attribution; if evidence already has standing and the function depends on handling it, prefer operation_function.
If the operation itself performs the conversion, prefer operation_function; if it mainly relates multiple warrants, prefer warrant_relation.
If the local operation produces the function, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
If the function is produced by local operation, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
Ask whether the function depends on the act of confessing/testifying or on whose perspective/testimonial role controls the annotation.

operation_function.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
operation_function.definition: A visible interpretive, testimonial, consultative, recognitional, or self-reading operation converts evidence into the primary interpretation.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for any action; the action must perform interpretive conversion.
Do not use when the operation only grants standing to one warrant; consider warrant_attribution.
Do not use when later framing does the reclassification; use temporal_layering.
operation_function.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: The act is physical but not interpretive.
The operation is mentioned but not needed for the decision.
positive_indicators: A character interprets a sign and that act supplies the function.
A confession or recognition changes how evidence is read.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the visible operation were removed but the same evidence remained, would the proposed interpretation still follow?

operation_function.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and teachable rule.
trigger: visible operation converts evidence to function

use_another_value_when: Use warrant_attribution when authority assigned to one source is dominant.
Use warrant_relation when the operation primarily ranks or relates multiple warrants.
Use perspective_assignment when speaker position or standpoint controls the function.
use_when: Someone interprets, recognizes, confesses, testifies, consults, translates, classifies, or self-reads in a way that converts evidence.
The operation is visible in the source packet.
Removing the operation would change the interpretation.

perspective_assignment.analytic_question: Would changing the speaker, witness position, or standpoint change the interpretation?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: Speaker identity is stable and not consequential.
The perspective is only one warrant among multiple ranked warrants.
candidate_detection.question: Does stable attachment plus standpoint, focalization, witness role, or epistemic position plausibly control interpretation?
supported_if: The same statement functions differently because it is internal focalization rather than public report.
A witness role changes whether evidence counts as observation or rumor.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the same words or observations came from a different standpoint or witness role with attachment held stable, would the proposed interpretation change?
Ask whether incompatibility among multiple warrants determines the function, or whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation.
Ask whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation; if incompatibility among multiple warrants is dominant, use warrant_relation.
Ask whether the function depends on the act of confessing/testifying or on whose perspective/testimonial role controls the annotation.
Hold standpoint constant and alter unit or speaker attachment; then hold attachment constant and alter standpoint. Which manipulation changes the result?
If the same source retains authority but standpoint changes, does interpretation change; if standpoint stays constant but authority is withdrawn, does support fail?

perspective_assignment.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
perspective_assignment.definition: Whose perspective, witness role, testimonial position, focalization, or epistemic standpoint determines the function.
do_not_use_when: Do not use merely because a speaker is present.
Do not use when attachment or unit boundary is unstable; use boundary_setting.
Do not use when multiple accounts interact as warrants; use warrant_relation.
perspective_assignment.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: Speaker identity is stable and not consequential.
The perspective is only one warrant among multiple ranked warrants.
positive_indicators: The same statement functions differently because it is internal focalization rather than public report.
A witness role changes whether evidence counts as observation or rumor.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the same words or observations came from a different standpoint or witness role with attachment held stable, would the proposed interpretation change?

perspective_assignment.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and defensible lineage.
trigger: standpoint, focalization, witness role, or testimonial position controls function

use_another_value_when: Use boundary_setting for uncertain attachment, scope, or unit boundary.
Use warrant_attribution when one speaker or source receives standing or authority.
Use warrant_relation when two or more accounts interact, conflict, rank, or qualify each other.
use_when: The function depends on who sees, speaks, remembers, reports, or frames the evidence.
Attachment is stable, but standpoint or epistemic position changes interpretation.
Changing standpoint would change the primary interpretation.

temporal_layering.analytic_question: Does a later or retrospective layer reclassify what earlier evidence does?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: Chronology is present but not interpretively active.
A later fact is simply another warrant in a conflict.
candidate_detection.question: Does a later, retrospective, reuse, fulfilment, or historical-readability layer plausibly reclassify earlier evidence?
supported_if: A later fulfilment recasts an earlier sign.
A retrospective frame changes earlier evidence from incidental to functional.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the later or retrospective layer were removed, would the earlier evidence keep the same function?
If removing cross-warrant conflict removes the function, prefer warrant_relation; if removing the later temporal frame removes it, prefer temporal_layering.
If the local operation produces the function, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
If removing the later temporal frame removes the function, prefer temporal_layering; if removing cross-warrant conflict removes it, prefer warrant_relation.
If the function is produced by local operation, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
Ask whether later readability is the decisive feature; if yes, use temporal_layering rather than context_inference.

temporal_layering.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
temporal_layering.definition: Later fulfilment, retrospective framing, historical readability, or reuse across time changes the function of earlier evidence.
do_not_use_when: Do not use merely because events happen in sequence.
Do not use when a local recognition act performs conversion; use operation_function.
Do not use when multiple warrants conflict without time-layered reclassification; use warrant_relation.
temporal_layering.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: Chronology is present but not interpretively active.
A later fact is simply another warrant in a conflict.
positive_indicators: A later fulfilment recasts an earlier sign.
A retrospective frame changes earlier evidence from incidental to functional.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the later or retrospective layer were removed, would the earlier evidence keep the same function?

temporal_layering.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and clear lineage.
trigger: later, retrospective, reuse, or fulfilment layer changes earlier evidence function

use_another_value_when: Use operation_function when a visible local act converts evidence.
Use warrant_relation when conflict among warrants is dominant.
Use context_inference when historical background is external rather than a textual time layer.
use_when: A later event changes how earlier evidence functions.
The record depends on retrospective framing or later readability.
The same evidence has one local role and another later role.

warrant_attribution.analytic_question: Does the decision turn on whether one item is allowed to count as a warrant?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: Several sources interact and none singly carries the decision.
The source’s standing is stable and another locus explains conversion.
candidate_detection.question: Does one source, speaker, medium, sign, or result plausibly receive warranting force?
supported_if: One speaker or sign is treated as decisive.
One medium or source is granted authority over alternatives.

counterfactual_tests.question: If this single source, speaker, medium, sign, or result lost warranting standing, would the proposed interpretation lose its support?
Ask whether the cue type itself is underdetermined after excluding more specific loci, or whether one source, sign, medium, speaker, or result is granted standing.
Count the warrants: if the annotation depends on how more than one warrant interacts, use warrant_relation; if one source receives authority, use warrant_attribution.
If evidence becomes usable as a warrant through the operation, prefer warrant_attribution; if evidence already has standing and the function depends on handling it, prefer operation_function.
If the same source retains authority but standpoint changes, does interpretation change; if standpoint stays constant but authority is withdrawn, does support fail?

warrant_attribution.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
warrant_attribution.definition: One source, medium, speaker, sign, or result is granted warranting force for the primary interpretation.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when multiple warrants must be ranked or reconciled; use warrant_relation.
Do not use when the visible operation itself converts evidence; use operation_function.
Do not use when standpoint controls the function; use perspective_assignment.
warrant_attribution.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: Several sources interact and none singly carries the decision.
The source’s standing is stable and another locus explains conversion.
positive_indicators: One speaker or sign is treated as decisive.
One medium or source is granted authority over alternatives.

primary_counterfactual.question: If this single source, speaker, medium, sign, or result lost warranting standing, would the proposed interpretation lose its support?

warrant_attribution.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and relatively clear lineage.
trigger: single source, medium, speaker, sign, or result receives warranting force

use_another_value_when: Use warrant_relation when two or more warrants interact.
Use operation_function when a visible interpretive or testimonial operation performs conversion.
Use perspective_assignment when witness position or focalization controls the interpretation.
use_when: A single source, speaker, medium, sign, result, or textual item receives standing.
Removing that item’s standing would change the interpretation.
The dominant issue is attribution of warranting force rather than interaction among multiple warrants.

warrant_relation.analytic_question: Does the interpretation depend on how two or more warrants interact?

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: Only one warrant is operative.
The relation is incidental and another locus performs conversion.
candidate_detection.question: Do two or more local warrants plausibly interact, conflict, rank, qualify, or suspend each other?
supported_if: Two accounts conflict and the record depends on their relation.
One warrant qualifies or suspends another.

counterfactual_tests.question: If the same warrants no longer conflicted, ranked, qualified, suspended, or interacted, would the primary interpretation change?
Count the warrants: if the annotation depends on how more than one warrant interacts, use warrant_relation; if one source receives authority, use warrant_attribution.
If removing cross-warrant conflict removes the function, prefer warrant_relation; if removing the later temporal frame removes it, prefer temporal_layering.
Ask whether incompatibility among multiple warrants determines the function, or whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation.
If the operation itself performs the conversion, prefer operation_function; if it mainly relates multiple warrants, prefer warrant_relation.
Ask whether changing the boundary of the annotation would change the result; if the boundary is stable but warrants interact, use warrant_relation.
If removing the later temporal frame removes the function, prefer temporal_layering; if removing cross-warrant conflict removes it, prefer warrant_relation.
Ask whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation; if incompatibility among multiple warrants is dominant, use warrant_relation.
Ask whether the local text contains interacting warrants; if yes, use warrant_relation. Use context_inference only when the missing contextual bridge is dominant.

warrant_relation.decision_consequence: Record the proposed locus separately from operational status and final operational label; apply review policy before final analysis use.
warrant_relation.definition: Conflict, ranking, qualification, suspension, or interaction among multiple warrants is the dominant procedural locus.
do_not_use_when: Do not use merely because records disagree.
Do not use when one source alone is granted standing; use warrant_attribution.
Do not use when later framing reclassifies earlier evidence; use temporal_layering.
warrant_relation.escalation_condition: Escalate if primary or pairwise counterfactual tests are inconclusive, contradictory, unsupported by evidence, or trigger the category review policy.
exclusion_indicators: The relation is incidental and another locus performs conversion.
positive_indicators: Two accounts conflict and the record depends on their relation.
One warrant qualifies or suspends another.

primary_counterfactual.question: If the same warrants no longer conflicted, ranked, qualified, suspended, or interacted, would the primary interpretation change?

warrant_relation.provenance_note: Active lineage and predicted-confusion sources are inherited where possible; candidate-detection, dominance, and governance details are declared v0.1 synthesis.

review_policy.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and defensible relation to argumentation theory.
trigger: multiple warrants interact, conflict, rank, qualify, or suspend each other

use_another_value_when: Use warrant_attribution for one source receiving standing.
Use temporal_layering when later or retrospective framing changes earlier evidence.
Use perspective_assignment when standpoint, not cross-warrant relation, controls the function.
use_when: Multiple local warrants point in different or mutually qualifying directions.
The record ranks, reconciles, suspends, combines, or contrasts warrants.
Removing the relation among warrants would change the interpretation.

claim_boundary: Manual repair draft for protocol audit; provisional audit vocabulary, no empirical validation, no gold-standard claim.

counterfactual_test_protocol.conflict_policy: Conflicting tests produce final_operational_label unresolved and escalation_required true.
mandatory_when: two or more categories are candidate_supported

insufficient_evidence_if: selected evidence or rationale is too thin to evaluate this locus
not_supported_if: A more specific locus remains supported.
The cue is only topic vocabulary.

stage_A_candidate_detection.evidence_sufficiency_question: Is there enough recorded evidence and rationale to identify and compare plausible procedural loci?

stage_B_dominance_resolution.order_independence_rule: Candidate checks are evaluated as a set. Category order must not determine the final locus.

rules.action: Apply that category primary counterfactual test. If supported, retain as proposed_locus and apply review policy. If inconclusive or contradictory, final_operational_label unresolved and escalation_required true.

Apply relevant pairwise disambiguation test and both removal/substitution counterfactuals where relevant. Choose dominant locus only when one side is better supported; otherwise unresolved with escalation.
Apply pairwise tests needed to compare plausible candidates. Do not use tournament ordering. If no stable dominant locus emerges, unresolved with escalation.

Apply category-specific review policy. Model proposals requiring review preserve proposed_locus but set operational_status requires_human_review, final_operational_label unresolved, escalation_required true.

evidence_before_locus_rule: Record evidence and rationale before assigning friction_locus. Ask whether there is enough recorded evidence and rationale to identify and compare plausible procedural loci; do not ask whether the primary interpretation is fully licensed.
global_coding_sequence: evaluate candidate detection for all eight categories without early termination
apply relevant primary and pairwise counterfactual tests
record proposed_locus, operational_status, final_operational_label, uncertainty, alternatives, and escalation separately

fields.final_operational_label: The label admitted for analysis after applying review policy. This may be unresolved even when proposed_locus is substantive.
fields.operational_status: Status for analysis use: accepted_for_analysis, requires_human_review, unresolved, or not_supplied.
fields.original_record_preserved: The original proposal remains unchanged; later review creates a separate review record.
fields.proposed_locus: The substantive locus proposed by the coder or model before governance review.
fields.review_policy: Category-specific governance rule applied to proposed_locus.

rules: Human proposal, model proposal, and post-review decision are distinct events.
A model proposal never autonomously approves a review-required value.

For model-proposed review-required categories preserve proposed_locus, set operational_status requires_human_review, set final_operational_label unresolved, set escalation_required true, and create any later review as a separate record.
unresolved as final_operational_label is distinct from a substantive proposed_locus.

one_dominant_locus_rule.secondary_loci: candidate_loci and alternatives may be recorded in decision_path/counterfactual_tests; do not encode multiple final labels

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether the problem is what this cue type does here, or what an act of interpretation, consultation, confession, recognition, or self-reading does.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Reserved cue_function may be overused when operation_function is the more specific locus.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Cue-family uncertainty concerns what kind of cue is operating; operation_function concerns a visible act performed with or on the cue.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: If removing the act removes conversion, operation_function wins; if changing cue family while the act stays constant changes conversion, cue_function remains plausible.
pairwise_disambiguation.prior_examples: operation_function has prior positive examples; cue_function has no positive demo example
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Holding cue content constant, does removing the act of interpreting/consulting/recognizing remove the conversion?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: A cue type such as prophecy, testimony, or divination may be mistaken for the visible act performed with it.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether the cue type itself is underdetermined after excluding more specific loci, or whether one source, sign, medium, speaker, or result is granted standing.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Reserved cue_function may be overused when warrant_attribution is the more specific locus.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Cue function concerns cue-family role; warrant_attribution concerns one source, sign, speaker, medium, or result receiving standing.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: If standing is decisive, warrant_attribution wins; if standing is stable and cue family remains unstable, cue_function remains plausible.
pairwise_disambiguation.prior_examples: warrant_attribution has prior positive examples; cue_function has no positive demo example
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Holding cue family constant, does loss of warranting standing remove support?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: A cue family may be confused with the source or medium receiving warranting force.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Count the warrants: if the annotation depends on how more than one warrant interacts, use warrant_relation; if one source receives authority, use warrant_attribution.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: One-warrant cases may be overcoded as warrant_relation when contextual complexity is present.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: One source receives standing versus two or more warrants interacting, ranking, qualifying, or conflicting.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: One decisive warrant supports warrant_attribution; interacting warrants support warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Count the operative warrants: would removing interaction among warrants change the interpretation?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: A single source granted authority may be confused with an interaction among multiple warrants.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If evidence becomes usable as a warrant through the operation, prefer warrant_attribution; if evidence already has standing and the function depends on handling it, prefer operation_function.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Warrant attribution may be overcoded as operation_function when interpretation is visibly present.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Authority assigned to one source versus a visible operation performing conversion.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: If standing removal is decisive, warrant_attribution wins; if operation removal is decisive, operation_function wins; if both are necessary and neither dominates, unresolved.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Apply two manipulations: remove warranting standing while preserving the operation; then remove the operation while granting standing in advance.
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Authority is often assigned through an interpretive act, so granting standing may be confused with visible operation.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If removing cross-warrant conflict removes the function, prefer warrant_relation; if removing the later temporal frame removes it, prefer temporal_layering.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Warrant conflicts in retrospectively framed cases may be mistaken for temporal_layering, or temporal effects may be flattened into warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Local warrants interacting versus a later or retrospective layer reclassifying earlier evidence.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Interaction decisive supports warrant_relation; later/reuse frame decisive supports temporal_layering.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Does removing cross-warrant interaction or removing the later temporal frame change the interpretation more directly?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Cross-warrant conflict may coexist with retrospective framing or later readability.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether incompatibility among multiple warrants determines the function, or whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Perspective-governed testimony may be overcoded as warrant_relation when multiple accounts are visible.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Two or more accounts interact, conflict, rank, or qualify each other versus one standpoint’s position changing function.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Account interaction supports warrant_relation; standpoint change supports perspective_assignment.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If standpoint is held constant, does warrant interaction still drive the decision; if warrant relation is held constant, does changing standpoint change function?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Incompatible testimony or standpoint can look like conflict among warrants or like perspective control.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If the operation itself performs the conversion, prefer operation_function; if it mainly relates multiple warrants, prefer warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Operations that rank warrants may be overcoded as operation_function when warrant_relation is dominant.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Operation performs conversion versus operation mainly creates or ranks relations among warrants.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Operation decisive supports operation_function; relation decisive supports warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If the operation is removed but warrant relation remains, does the function persist; if the warrant relation is removed but operation remains, does the function persist?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: An operation can matter because it performs conversion or because it creates, exposes, ranks, or intensifies relations among warrants.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If the local operation produces the function, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Later reclassification may be overcoded as operation_function when a recognition scene is visible.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Visible act converts evidence versus later time position or retrospective framing reclassifies evidence.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Act-removal decisive supports operation_function; later-frame removal decisive supports temporal_layering; both necessary without dominance means unresolved.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If the later time position remains but the interpretive act is removed, does reclassification still occur; if the act remains but later framing is removed, does reclassification still occur?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Recognition or fulfilment can be treated as a local operation or as later reclassification of earlier evidence.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether changing the boundary of the annotation would change the result; if the boundary is stable but warrants interact, use warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Boundary_setting may be underused when coders move directly to warrant_relation inside an unstable unit.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Changing unit, attachment, or scope changes result versus warrants interact inside a stable unit.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Boundary change decisive supports boundary_setting; warrant interaction decisive supports warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.prior_examples: warrant_relation has prior positive examples; boundary_setting has no positive demo example
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Hold warrant content constant and alter unit/scope; then hold unit/scope constant and alter warrant relation. Which change controls?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: A scope or unit boundary may be confused with the relation among warrants inside a stable unit.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether the relevant unit/scope is unstable, or whether the unit is stable but needs external contextual inference to support conversion.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Boundary problems may be absorbed into context_inference as a residual explanation.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Changing the unit or scope changes the result versus unit is stable and a named contextual bridge is required.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Unit/scope change supports boundary_setting; stable unit plus named bridge supports context_inference.
pairwise_disambiguation.prior_examples: neither boundary_setting nor context_inference has a positive demo example in the cited manual lineage
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If unit/scope is fixed differently does result change; if unit is stable, can a named contextual bridge be cited as necessary?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: A missing boundary may be confused with a missing contextual bridge.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If removing the later temporal frame removes the function, prefer temporal_layering; if removing cross-warrant conflict removes it, prefer warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Temporal_layering may be undercoded when later readability appears alongside multiple warrants.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Later/reuse layer reclassifies earlier evidence versus local warrant interaction.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Later layer decisive supports temporal_layering; interaction decisive supports warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Does removing the later layer or removing warrant interaction change the decision more directly?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Later fulfilment, retrospective readability, or reuse may coexist with local warrant conflict.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If the function is produced by local operation, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Temporal effects may be overcoded as operation_function when the text includes a visible recognition act.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Temporal reclassification versus visible local operation.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Later-frame removal decisive supports temporal_layering; act removal decisive supports operation_function.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If the act remains but later framing is removed, and if later framing remains but the act is removed, which removal changes the decision?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: A visible recognition or fulfilment scene can look like a local operation.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation; if incompatibility among multiple warrants is dominant, use warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Perspective_assignment may be undercoded when multiple accounts make warrant conflict salient.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: One standpoint’s position changes function versus two or more accounts interact, conflict, rank, or qualify each other.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Standpoint decisive supports perspective_assignment; account interaction decisive supports warrant_relation.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Does changing standpoint alone change function, or does the relation among accounts do the work?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Perspective, witness role, or testimonial position may be confused with incompatibility among multiple accounts as warrants.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether the function depends on the act of confessing/testifying or on whose perspective/testimonial role controls the annotation.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Perspective-governed testimony may be overcoded as operation_function when the speech act is prominent.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Standpoint controls interpretation versus a speech/reading/confession/testimony act performs conversion.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Standpoint change supports perspective_assignment; act removal supports operation_function.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If the act remains but standpoint changes, and if standpoint remains but act is removed, which manipulation controls?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Confession or testimony can be treated as a visible operation or as standpoint-governed perspective.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether later readability is the decisive feature; if yes, use temporal_layering rather than context_inference.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Context_inference may be overused when temporal_layering provides the more specific explanation.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: External or non-local contextual bridge versus later/reuse layer in the text.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Named external/non-local bridge supports context_inference; later textual layer supports temporal_layering.
pairwise_disambiguation.prior_examples: temporal_layering has prior positive examples; context_inference has no positive demo example
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Is the needed bridge documented as context outside the local anchor, or is reclassification produced by a later textual layer?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Historical readability or later framing may be mistaken for external contextual inference.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Ask whether the local text contains interacting warrants; if yes, use warrant_relation. Use context_inference only when the missing contextual bridge is dominant.
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Context_inference may be overused as a residual category for difficult warrant interactions.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: External or non-local bridge licenses conversion versus locally available warrants interact.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: If local interaction suffices, warrant_relation wins; if a named bridge is necessary and permitted, context_inference wins.
pairwise_disambiguation.prior_examples: warrant_relation has prior positive examples; context_inference has no positive demo example
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Can locally available warrants explain conversion without the contextual bridge?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: External or background context may be confused with interaction among warrants within the text.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: Hold standpoint constant and alter unit or speaker attachment; then hold attachment constant and alter standpoint. Which manipulation changes the result?
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Unresolved or wrong-locus risk if attachment, standpoint, and authority are not separated.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Uncertain attachment, scope, or unit boundary versus stable attachment with standpoint, focalization, witness role, or epistemic position changing interpretation.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Attachment/scope change supports boundary_setting; standpoint change with stable attachment supports perspective_assignment.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: Hold standpoint constant and alter unit or speaker attachment; then hold attachment constant and alter standpoint. Which manipulation changes the result?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Added in v0.1 repair because audit identified this distinction as operationally necessary.

pairwise_disambiguation.counterfactual_manipulation: If the same source retains authority but standpoint changes, does interpretation change; if standpoint stays constant but authority is withdrawn, does support fail?
pairwise_disambiguation.directional_error_risk: Unresolved or wrong-locus risk if attachment, standpoint, and authority are not separated.
pairwise_disambiguation.distinction: Standpoint or witness position controls interpretation versus one speaker or source receives standing or authority.
pairwise_disambiguation.pattern: Standpoint change supports perspective_assignment; authority withdrawal supports warrant_attribution.
pairwise_disambiguation.question: If the same source retains authority but standpoint changes, does interpretation change; if standpoint stays constant but authority is withdrawn, does support fail?
pairwise_disambiguation.unresolved_condition: If observable manipulations support both sides, neither side, or require unavailable evidence, set final_operational_label to unresolved and set escalation_required true.
pairwise_disambiguation.why_confusion_expected: Added in v0.1 repair because audit identified this distinction as operationally necessary.

worked_examples.alternative_pathway: It could be a mere informational notice if the signature had no standing.
worked_examples.focal_interpretive_decision: Does the notice function as authorization?
worked_examples.primary_counterfactual_test: If this single source, speaker, medium, sign, or result lost warranting standing, would the proposed interpretation lose its support?
worked_examples.primary_test_answer: supports_category: without the clerk signature standing, authorization is unsupported.
worked_examples.provenance: artificial minimal example; not PR #18 material

review_policy_applied.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and relatively clear lineage.
trigger: single source, medium, speaker, sign, or result receives warranting force

selected_evidence.text: Artificial packet: A notice bears a named clerk signature, and the local rule says clerk signatures authorize entry.

worked_examples.alternative_pathway: The clerk could be treated as an authority whose standing alone makes the token count.
worked_examples.focal_interpretive_decision: Does the token function as an order?
worked_examples.pairwise_counterfactual_test: Apply two manipulations: remove warranting standing while preserving the operation; then remove the operation while granting standing in advance.
worked_examples.pairwise_test_answer: supports_operation_function: removing warranting standing while preserving the reading still leaves the act as conversion in the packet; removing the operation while granting standing in advance removes the observed conversion.
worked_examples.primary_counterfactual_test: If the visible operation were removed but the same evidence remained, would the proposed interpretation still follow?
worked_examples.primary_test_answer: supports_category: without the reading act, the token does not become an order.
worked_examples.proposed_primary_interpretation: The token functions as an order because the clerk interprets it aloud.
worked_examples.provenance: artificial minimal example; not PR #18 material

review_policy_applied.source_basis: Lineage table says prior positive examples and teachable rule.
trigger: visible operation converts evidence to function

selected_evidence.text: Artificial packet: A clerk reads an unsigned token aloud and declares its meaning under a rule already accepted by all parties.

worked_examples.alternative_pathway: The mark might be a sign with warranting standing if a rule of authority were present, but no such rule is cited.
worked_examples.effect_on_decision: Preserve proposed_locus cue_function but do not admit it as final without review.
worked_examples.focal_interpretive_decision: Does the blue mark function as warning or authorization?
worked_examples.pairwise_counterfactual_test: not_applicable: no more specific candidate supported.
worked_examples.primary_counterfactual_test: If the same local content arrived through a different cue family, with speaker, operation, warrant, time, boundary, and context held stable, would the proposed interpretation change?
worked_examples.primary_test_answer: supports_category but review policy applies: changing cue family would change interpretation, yet cue_function is reserved.
worked_examples.proposed_primary_interpretation: The mark functions as a cue whose family is unresolved.
worked_examples.provenance: artificial minimal example; not PR #18 material

review_policy_applied.source_basis: Lineage table calls cue_function a provisional reserved value with no positive demo example.
trigger: cue family remains plausible after all more specific loci are tested
proposal is made by a model record
counterfactual tests do not support another specific locus

selected_evidence.text: Artificial packet: An unexplained blue mark appears; no speaker, operation, warrant relation, time layer, boundary issue, perspective, or contextual bridge is supported.

worked_examples.alternative_pathway: No complete alternative can be evaluated because evidence and rationale are insufficient.
worked_examples.effect_on_decision: Use unresolved and escalate for insufficient evidence.
worked_examples.focal_interpretive_decision: Cannot be identified from the record.
worked_examples.primary_counterfactual_test: Is there enough recorded evidence and rationale to identify and compare plausible procedural loci?
worked_examples.proposed_primary_interpretation: No responsible primary interpretation is supplied.
worked_examples.provenance: artificial minimal example; not PR #18 material

selected_evidence.text: Artificial packet: A sentence is fragmentary and the rationale gives no stable interpretive decision.

worked_examples.alternative_pathway: The promise reading could be produced by the announcement as an operation or by the later temporal position as reclassification.
decision_path: pairwise test cannot separate act from later frame
worked_examples.effect_on_decision: Do not force a winner; use unresolved and escalate.
worked_examples.focal_interpretive_decision: Does the earlier phrase function as a promise?
worked_examples.pairwise_counterfactual_test: If the later time position remains but the interpretive act is removed, does reclassification still occur; if the act remains but later framing is removed, does reclassification still occur?
worked_examples.pairwise_test_answer: conflicting: later framing and recognition act are not separable in the artificial packet.
worked_examples.primary_counterfactual_test: If the visible operation were removed but the same evidence remained, would the proposed interpretation still follow?
worked_examples.primary_test_answer: supports_category: removing the announcement act weakens conversion.
worked_examples.proposed_primary_interpretation: The earlier phrase may function as a promise.
worked_examples.provenance: artificial minimal example; not PR #18 material

selected_evidence.text: Artificial packet: A reader later announces that an earlier phrase was a promise, and the announcement itself is also the only visible act of recognition.
